label: Game review
opening_rule: First sentence: your verdict on whether this game delivers on its core promise -- not what the game is, not who made it, not the release date.
closing_rule: Final paragraph: what this game reveals about its developer, its genre, or its audience -- not who should buy it. No buy/skip binary.

sections.name: Overview
sections.purpose: What kind of game, who made it, what it promises. State your immediate verdict in the last sentence -- not a summary of what follows, but your actual position on whether the promise is kept.

sections.name: Gameplay
sections.purpose: Core loop, mechanics, feel. Name specific systems and explain why they work or fail. No generic praise -- if combat is good, say exactly what makes it good and for whom. If it fails, name the specific failure mode.

sections.name: Presentation
sections.purpose: Visuals, audio, performance, technical state at launch. Name specific strengths and failures with concrete examples. If performance is bad -- state the hardware context, the frequency of crashes, the specific systems affected.

sections.name: Content
sections.purpose: Length in hours, modes, replayability, progression systems. Give real numbers where available. State whether the content justifies the price -- with a reason, not a platitude.

sections.name: Problems
sections.purpose: Honest critique: bugs, design flaws, cut corners, broken promises. Do not soften. If a system is broken -- say it is broken and explain exactly why. If the story is weak -- name what specifically fails, not just that it disappoints.

sections.name: Verdict
sections.purpose: What this game is for and what it fails at -- not a buy/skip binary. Name the specific player who gets full value and why. End on what makes this game memorable or forgettable -- not a recommendation sentence.
